mainText:
### The SEC’s Proposed IPO Fix

#### DOJ Puts Weaponization Fund on Hold
On Monday, the Department of Justice announced it would comply with an order from U.S. District Judge Leonie Brinkema temporarily blocking the administration from setting up or operating its proposed $1.776 billion "anti-weaponization fund," pending a June 12 hearing for a lawsuit brought by progressive advocacy group Democracy Forward alleging the fund is illegal. The same day, the White House reportedly told Republican congressional leaders that it would stop trying to move the fund through Congress as part of an upcoming reconciliation package. The Trump administration had originally wanted the fund passed as part of a $70 billion immigration-enforcement package, but universal opposition from Democrats and strong pushback from Republicans delayed a Senate vote last month. Trump met with House Speaker Mike Johnson, a Republican from Louisiana, for three hours and reportedly discussed the settlement fund.

* U.S. District Judge Kathleen Williams, who oversaw the lawsuit brought by Trump against the IRS that resulted in the out-of-court settlement creating the fund, said she would open an inquiry into the case over concerns that Trump’s legal team had potentially filed "a frivolous lawsuit for the sole purpose of forcing a settlement."
* Senate Democrats are planning to block the proposed fund through amendments to the current reconciliation bill and force floor votes, according to a memo from Minority Leader Chuck Schumer. Three Democratic senators introduced a bill to eliminate the fund on Monday.

#### Ceasefire Negotiations Wobble Over Israeli Strikes on Lebanon
Iran said Monday that it was breaking off ceasefire negotiations with the U.S. due to recent Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon. President Donald Trump at first denied knowledge of the Iranian ultimatum, only to later post on Truth Social that he had held a "very productive" call with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on easing hostilities in Lebanon and plans to strike Beirut. Trump also claimed that he had communicated with Hezbollah, the Iran-backed terrorist group, through intermediaries and secured agreements from both sides not to attack each other. According to a report from Axios, Trump told the Israeli leader that he was "f—ing crazy" and that "everybody hates Israel because of this."
* Netanyahu tweeted on Monday that he told Trump during the call that "if Hezbollah does not cease attacking our cities and citizens—Israel will attack terror targets in Beirut."
* In a Monday statement, the Lebanese embassy in Washington, D.C., claimed that the U.S. proposal stipulated that "Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs would cease in exchange for Hezbollah refraining from carrying out attacks against Israel, with the ceasefire framework to be expanded to encompass all Lebanese territories." It also said further talks would be held on Tuesday and Wednesday.

#### Anthropic Files Paperwork for IPO
AI company Anthropic confidentially submitted a draft S-1 registration statement to the Securities and Exchange Commission on Monday for a proposed initial public offering. The company made the filing under Rule 135, allowing it to privately refine its disclosures before committing to a public listing, which is expected to launch as early as this fall. "The number of shares to be offered and the price have not yet been set," Anthropic wrote in a blog post announcing the move. In a funding round last week, Anthropic raised $65 billion at a $965 billion valuation, marking the first time it has ever been valued higher than its main competitor, OpenAI, which is valued at $852 billion. The jump tracks a surge in revenue: Anthropic’s annualized revenue has reached $47 billion, far surpassing OpenAI’s reported $30 billion.
* OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in an interview Monday that his company was not focused on the timing of a potential IPO.
* Elon Musk’s SpaceX, which merged with his xAI company in February, is set to go public next week, in what is expected to be the largest IPO ever. The company is reportedly seeking a valuation of at least $1.8 trillion.
